title: Manually Exchange a Tote by Tote ID
summary: Use the tote management Exchange Tote function to manually trigger a tote exchange for a specific tote by entering its tote ID. The training states that any tote on the system can be manually exchanged and shows Exchange Tote as a tote management API feature.
when_to_use: Use when a knowledgeable support user needs to manually trigger a tote exchange for a specific tote and the tote ID is known.
not_for: Do not use when the tote ID cannot be confirmed from the available system information.
Do not use as a fully validated recovery workflow; the source presents the function and its purpose but does not define detailed safeguards or validation steps.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify the correct tote ID, open the tote management Exchange Tote function, submit the exchange request, and verify the system response or tote status.

safety_notes: This is a live state-changing action.
The source states the function can be used for testing, but does not provide additional safeguards, approval requirements, or validation controls.
Do not proceed if the tote ID cannot be confidently confirmed.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to tote management interface or API-backed Exchange Tote function
Ability to review tote status or task response

access_required: Access to the tote management interface or API page that includes Exchange Tote
system_preconditions: The tote to be exchanged is identifiable by tote ID in the system
The user can review tote status or system response after submitting the exchange

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the tote management API features, including Exchange Tote.

steps.title: Identify the tote ID
steps.purpose: Ensure the manual exchange is targeted to the correct tote.
steps.instruction: Identify and confirm the tote ID for the tote that needs to be manually exchanged before opening the Exchange Tote function.
steps.expected_result: A specific tote ID is available for entry into the Exchange Tote function.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote ID is known and can be used for the manual exchange action.
steps.failure_condition: The tote ID cannot be confirmed or there is uncertainty about which tote should be exchanged.

actions.label: Confirm tote ID
actions.exact_action: Identify the tote ID for the tote that needs to be manually exchanged.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the tote ID cannot be confirmed.

source_evidence.text: Identify the tote ID for the tote that needs to be manually exchanged.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Exchange Tote — Manually trigger a tote exchange by tote Id.

steps.title: Open the Exchange Tote function
steps.purpose: Navigate to the tote management function used to manually trigger the exchange.
steps.instruction: Open the tote management interface or API page and select the Exchange Tote function.
steps.expected_result: The Exchange Tote function is open and ready for tote ID entry.
steps.healthy_condition: The tote management page shows Exchange Tote as an available function and the user is positioned to perform the action.
steps.failure_condition: The Exchange Tote function is not available or the user cannot access the tote management page.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management API page listing Exchange Tote among the available tote management features.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the Exchange Tote function is not visible or accessible from the tote management page.

source_evidence.text: Open the Exchange Tote function. [Image support needed: training slide or interface view showing the Exchange Tote function in the tote management API page.]

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Aveva API page lists API Features for Tote Management: Add Tote, Remove Tote, Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.
Training slide showing API features for tote management, including Exchange Tote.

steps.title: Enter the tote ID
steps.purpose: Specify which tote should be manually exchanged.
steps.instruction: Enter the confirmed tote ID into the Exchange Tote function.
steps.expected_result: The Exchange Tote function contains the tote ID for the tote to be exchanged.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct tote ID is entered and ready for submission.
steps.failure_condition: The tote ID is missing, incorrect, or cannot be entered.

actions.label: Enter tote ID
actions.exact_action: Enter the tote ID for the tote to exchange.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The Exchange Tote function on the tote management page, which the training describes as operating by tote ID.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if there is uncertainty that the entered tote ID matches the intended tote.

source_evidence.text: Enter the tote ID for the tote to exchange.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: OCR: Exchange Tote — Manually trigger a tote exchange by tote Id.

steps.title: Submit the manual exchange
steps.purpose: Trigger the manual tote exchange for the specified tote.
steps.instruction: Submit the Exchange Tote action after entering the tote ID.
steps.expected_result: A manual tote exchange is triggered for the specified tote ID.
steps.healthy_condition: The system accepts the request and the exchange action is triggered.
steps.failure_condition: The exchange action does not complete or the tote remains in the original state.
stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the exchange action does not complete or the tote remains in the original state.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: You can like any tote on the system, you can manually exchange.
OCR: Exchange Tote — Manually trigger a tote exchange by tote Id.

steps.title: Verify the exchange was triggered
steps.purpose: Confirm that the requested manual exchange was accepted by the system.
steps.instruction: Verify through the available tote status or system response that the tote exchange was triggered.
steps.expected_result: The system response or tote status indicates that the manual exchange was triggered for the specified tote.
steps.healthy_condition: Available status or response shows the tote exchange was triggered.
steps.failure_condition: No confirming response is available, the exchange does not appear to have been triggered, or the tote remains in the original state.

actions.label: Check tote status or response
actions.exact_action: Verify through the available tote status or system response that the tote exchange was triggered.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The tote management page features such as Get Tote Task or related tote management functions that indicate available status or response review capability.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the exchange action does not complete or the tote remains in the original state.

source_evidence.text: Verify through the available tote status or system response that the tote exchange was triggered.
A manual tote exchange is triggered for the specified tote ID.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Visible functions include Get Tote Task, Resend Tote Task, Exchange Tote, Exchange Lane, and Get Exchange Settings.

success_criteria: The Exchange Tote function is accessed successfully.
The intended tote ID is entered.
The manual exchange action is submitted.
Available tote status or system response indicates the exchange was triggered for the specified tote.
healthy_conditions: The tote management page includes the Exchange Tote function.
The correct tote ID is known before submission.
The system accepts the manual exchange request.
Post-action status or response indicates the exchange was triggered.
failure_conditions: The tote ID cannot be confirmed.
The Exchange Tote function cannot be accessed.
The exchange action does not complete.
The tote remains in the original state after submission.
The source does not provide detailed safeguards or validation steps beyond basic status/response verification.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the tote ID cannot be confirmed.
Escalate if the exchange action does not complete or the tote remains in the original state.
Escalate to SME review if additional safeguards, approvals, or validation steps are required because the source does not define them.
